primary_written_source.citation: "Lamborghini Countach Road Test." Road & Track, February 1976.
primary_written_source.notes: Recorded 0-60 in 6.8 sec, ~163 mph top speed. Titled it 'Fastest Car We've Ever Tested.' The actual magazine scans are included in the magazine/ folder.

artifact_types.period-artifact: Contemporaneous artifact from the primary source era — actual magazine page scans, factory press photographs, period advertising, celebrity-delivery news photos, auction-catalog documentation. Cite per its own provenance.
artifact_types.specimen-photograph: Modern photograph of the original vehicle, typically from a motor show, museum, or collection display. Supplements period artifacts by showing the physical car today.

image_primary_sources.creator: Lamborghini factory / Walter Wolf archive
image_primary_sources.description: Walter Wolf with his first Countach LP400 Speciale (chassis 1120148) — the red-on-black racing-livery car Wolf commissioned Lamborghini to build as the first private owner's special. The Wolf cars pioneered the wide-body, flared-arch, rear-wing aesthetic that would define the LP400S production car.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Walter Wolf with his Countach LP400 Speciale, c. 1976, via LamboCars, https://www.lambocars.com/walter-wolf-specials/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Lamborghini / Walter Wolf archive. Walter Wolf with Countach LP400 Speciale. Photograph. c. 1976. Via LamboCars. https://www.lambocars.com/walter-wolf-specials/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Walter Wolf with Countach LP400 Speciale, c. 1976, LamboCars archive.

image_primary_sources.creator: RM Sotheby's auction catalog photography
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — auction catalog photo
image_primary_sources.description: The white 1989 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary (chassis KLA12722) driven by Leonardo DiCaprio in *The Wolf of Wall Street* (2013), auctioned by RM Sotheby's in December 2023 for $1.655 million — a record for the 25th Anniversary specification.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: RM Sotheby's, *1989 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary from The Wolf of Wall Street*, December 2023 auction catalog, https://rmsothebys.com/media-center/press-releases/one-of-two-the-wolf-of-wall-street-lamborghini-countachs-comes-to-market-iconic-wall-street-raging-bull-headlines-rm-sothebys-upcoming-new-york-sale/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: RM Sotheby's. *1989 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary from The Wolf of Wall Street*. Auction catalog photograph. December 2023. https://rmsothebys.com/media-center/press-releases/one-of-two-the-wolf-of-wall-street-lamborghini-countachs-comes-to-market-iconic-wall-street-raging-bull-headlines-rm-sothebys-upcoming-new-york-sale/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: RM Sotheby's, *Wolf of Wall Street Countach 25th Anniversary*.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:1980s Nuccio Bertone, Countach and Miura.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Unknown photographer
image_primary_sources.description: Nuccio Bertone with the Countach and its predecessor, the Miura — the two Lamborghinis his firm designed that defined the supercar idiom.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Unknown photographer, *Nuccio Bertone with the Countach and Miura*, c. 1985, Wikimedia Commons, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1980s_Nuccio_Bertone,_Countach_and_Miura.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Unknown photographer. *Nuccio Bertone with the Countach and Miura*. Photograph. c. 1985. Wikimedia Commons. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1980s_Nuccio_Bertone,_Countach_and_Miura.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Unknown photographer, *Nuccio Bertone with the Countach and Miura*.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:1970s Nuccio Bertone Stratos and Countach.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Jesse Alexander
image_primary_sources.description: Carrozzeria Bertone founder Nuccio Bertone in front of the Lancia Stratos and Lamborghini Countach, two of his firm's landmark wedge-era designs.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Jesse Alexander, *Nuccio Bertone with the Stratos and Countach*, c. 1974, Wikimedia Commons, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1970s_Nuccio_Bertone_Stratos_and_Countach.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Alexander, Jesse. *Nuccio Bertone with the Stratos and Countach*. Photograph. c. 1974. Wikimedia Commons. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1970s_Nuccio_Bertone_Stratos_and_Countach.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Alexander, *Nuccio Bertone with the Stratos and Countach*.

image_primary_sources.creator: Unknown photographer
image_primary_sources.description: Period portrait of Marcello Gandini, the Bertone designer who penned the Countach LP500 prototype and LP400 production car.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Unknown photographer, *Marcello Gandini in 1976*, 1976, Wikimedia Commons, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Marcello_Gandini_in_1976.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Unknown photographer. *Marcello Gandini in 1976*. Photograph. 1976. Wikimedia Commons. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Marcello_Gandini_in_1976.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Unknown photographer, *Marcello Gandini in 1976*, 1976, Wikimedia Commons.

image_primary_sources.creator: Road & Track magazine
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — magazine page scan
image_primary_sources.description: Scan of the first page of the Road & Track February 1976 road test of the Lamborghini Countach LP400 by Ron Wakefield — the exhibit's primary written source for the Countach.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: "Lamborghini Countach Road Test," *Road & Track*, February 1976, scanned and republished by Curbside Classic, https://www.curbsideclassic.com/vintage-reviews/road-track-vintage-road-test-1976-lamborghini-countach-fastest-car-weve-ever-tested/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: "Lamborghini Countach Road Test." *Road & Track*, February 1976. Page scan via Curbside Classic. https://www.curbsideclassic.com/vintage-reviews/road-track-vintage-road-test-1976-lamborghini-countach-fastest-car-weve-ever-tested/.

image_primary_sources.creator: Road & Track magazine
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — magazine page scan
image_primary_sources.description: Scan of a subsequent page of the Road & Track February 1976 Countach test showing specifications and photography.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: "Lamborghini Countach Road Test," *Road & Track*, February 1976, page 2 scan via Curbside Classic, https://www.curbsideclassic.com/vintage-reviews/road-track-vintage-road-test-1976-lamborghini-countach-fastest-car-weve-ever-tested/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: "Lamborghini Countach Road Test." *Road & Track*, February 1976. Page scan via Curbside Classic. https://www.curbsideclassic.com/vintage-reviews/road-track-vintage-road-test-1976-lamborghini-countach-fastest-car-weve-ever-tested/.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:1986 Pirelli Countach top view poster.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Pirelli
image_primary_sources.description: Pirelli factory advertising poster of the Countach in top-down view, showing the car's wedge silhouette and the P7 tires developed for it — a period primary source for how the Countach was marketed.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Pirelli, *Countach Top View Poster*, April 1986, Wikimedia Commons, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1986_Pirelli_Countach_top_view_poster.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Pirelli. *Countach Top View Poster*. Print advertisement. April 1986. Wikimedia Commons. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1986_Pirelli_Countach_top_view_poster.jpg.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:1974 Lamborghini Countach LP400 at Grand basel 2018 (Ank Kumar) 01.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Ank Kumar
image_primary_sources.description: An early production 1974 Lamborghini Countach LP400 — one of the first 150 LP400s built at Sant'Agata.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Ank Kumar, *1974 Lamborghini Countach LP400 at Grand Basel*, 2018, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1974_Lamborghini_Countach_LP400_at_Grand_basel_2018_(Ank_Kumar)_01.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Kumar, Ank. *1974 Lamborghini Countach LP400 at Grand Basel*. Photograph. 2018. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1974_Lamborghini_Countach_LP400_at_Grand_basel_2018_(Ank_Kumar)_01.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Kumar, *1974 Lamborghini Countach LP400 at Grand Basel*.

image_primary_sources.creator: Calreyn88
image_primary_sources.description: A 1975 Lamborghini Countach LP400 'Periscopio' — the earliest production specification tested by Road & Track for their February 1976 road test, distinguished by the roof periscope mirror.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Calreyn88, *1975 Lamborghini Countach LP400 Periscopo*, 2024, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1975_Lamborghini_Countach_LP400_Periscopo_2.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Calreyn88. *1975 Lamborghini Countach LP400 Periscopo*. Photograph. 2024. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1975_Lamborghini_Countach_LP400_Periscopo_2.jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: MrWalkr
image_primary_sources.description: A 1976 Lamborghini Countach LP400 in Periscopio specification — contemporaneous with the Road & Track February 1976 road test.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: MrWalkr, *1976 Lamborghini Countach LP400 Periscopio*, 2022, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1976_Lamborghini_Countach_LP400_Periscopio.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: MrWalkr. *1976 Lamborghini Countach LP400 Periscopio*. Photograph. 2022. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1976_Lamborghini_Countach_LP400_Periscopio.jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: Dave Hamster
image_primary_sources.description: The Countach LP500 prototype — the yellow Bertone-designed car unveiled at the 1971 Geneva Motor Show, reconstructed by Lamborghini. This is the design-schematic form of the car that Gandini drew.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Dave Hamster, *Lamborghini Countach LP500 Prototype*, 2016, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1982_Lamborghini_Countach_LP500.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Hamster, Dave. *Lamborghini Countach LP500 Prototype*. Photograph. 2016. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY 2.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1982_Lamborghini_Countach_LP500.jpg.

notes_on_use: Period artifacts (magazine scans, delivery photos, press kits) are used under fair-use educational exemption for this UTK museum exhibit.
Specimen photographs are Wikimedia Commons CC / PD images — attribution per the license strings.
All chicago_caption / chicago_bibliography strings are pre-formatted.
When rendering captions, link to source_page (not source_url) so the viewer lands on the article that provides provenance.
